produced_by: w2 machine — IO Core + IO Appendix deep audit

three_conditions_for_io_benefit_in_mss_c.summary: CrisPRO's definitive finding: IO benefit in MSS CRC requires ALL THREE conditions simultaneously. Missing any one condition predicts failure.

condition_1_non_liver_metastatic.what: Patient must NOT have liver metastases at enrollment

why_biologically.mechanism: The liver is an immunosuppressive organ. Kupffer cells (liver-resident macrophages) constitutively express PD-L1 and secrete IL-10 and TGF-β, creating a tolerogenic microenvironment. Liver metastases recruit regulatory T cells (Tregs) and myeloid-derived suppressor cells (MDSCs) that suppress CD8+ T cell activity systemically — not just locally. A patient with liver metastases has systemic immune suppression that checkpoint inhibitors cannot overcome.
clinical_evidence: CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (IO HARMFUL) vs no-LM PFS HR=0.54 (IO BENEFICIAL); Pint=0.02
REGONIVO US: 0/5 liver-met responders; non-liver-met ORR 22% (5/23)
REGONIVO Japan: all 8 CRC responders had NO liver metastases
RIN: NLM cohort ORR ~40% vs liver-met ORR ~5%
CAMILLA: post-hoc — patients without liver metastases had better outcomes
why_biologically.implication_for_trial_design: Liver metastasis status must be a pre-specified stratification variable. Trials that do not stratify by liver metastasis status will dilute the IO signal with immunosuppressed patients.

condition_1_non_liver_metastatic.prevalence_in_mss_mcrc: ~50–60% of mCRC patients have liver metastases at diagnosis. Restricting to NLM patients reduces the eligible population by ~50%.
condition_1_non_liver_metastatic.crispro_module: MOD-01 (Liver Metastasis Stratification) — EXECUTABLE_NOW — CRITICAL priority

condition_2_ptmb_28.what: Patient must have plasma TMB ≥28 mutations/Mb (GuardantOMNI assay)

why_biologically.mechanism: Tumor mutational burden (TMB) is a proxy for neoantigen load — the number of tumor-specific peptides that can be presented on MHC-I and recognized by T cells. High TMB tumors generate more neoantigens, creating more targets for checkpoint-released T cells. In MSS CRC, most tumors have low TMB — but the pTMB ≥28 subgroup (~15–20% of MSS mCRC) has sufficient neoantigen load for IO benefit.
why_biologically.why_plasma_not_tissue: Tissue TMB and plasma TMB are poorly correlated in CRC (Spearman r=0.13, p=0.20 — CO.26). Tissue TMB is confounded by tumor heterogeneity and archival tissue quality. Plasma TMB (pTMB) from ctDNA captures the mutational landscape of all metastatic sites simultaneously.
clinical_evidence: CO.26: pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — strongest IO signal from randomized data in MSS CRC
CO.26: pTMB vs tissue TMB Spearman r=0.13 (p=0.20) — tissue TMB is NOT a substitute
GRANITE GO-010: ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50 (directional benefit)
caveats: pTMB ≥28 threshold is data-driven (not pre-specified in CO.26 protocol)
GuardantOMNI-specific — not validated on other platforms
Derived from 2L+ refractory population — not validated in 1L

condition_2_ptmb_28.prevalence_in_mss_mcrc: ~15–20% of MSS mCRC patients have pTMB ≥28 (estimated from CO.26 subgroup)

condition_3_immune_inflamed_tme.what: Patient must have an immune-inflamed tumor microenvironment (high Immunoscore-IC, high CD8+ TIL density, low TGF-β suppression)

why_biologically.mechanism: Checkpoint inhibitors release the brakes on T cells — but T cells must already be present in the tumor to be released. MSS CRC tumors are predominantly 'immune excluded' or 'immune desert' — T cells are either blocked from entering the tumor or absent entirely. Only 'immune inflamed' tumors (T cells present at the tumor center and invasive margin) can respond to checkpoint blockade.

three_tme_phenotypes.immune_inflamed: CD8+ T cells present at tumor center AND invasive margin. High Immunoscore-IC. Responds to IO.
three_tme_phenotypes.immune_excluded: CD8+ T cells present at invasive margin but excluded from tumor center by stromal barriers (TGF-β, CAFs, decorin). Does not respond to IO alone — needs stromal barrier disruption.
three_tme_phenotypes.immune_desert: CD8+ T cells absent from both tumor center and invasive margin. Does not respond to IO.

clinical_evidence: AtezoTRIBE: Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 (exploratory) vs Immunoscore-IC low HR ~0.90
MEDITREME: CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup showed best outcomes
GVAX: T-cell responses confirmed in peripheral blood but ORR 0% — T cells activated but cannot infiltrate tumor (immune excluded phenotype)

condition_3_immune_inflamed_tme.crispro_module: MOD-05 (Immunoscore-IC / CD8+ Baseline) — EXECUTABLE_NOW — HIGH priority

pharmacodynamic_dissociation.definition: T-cell activation in peripheral blood WITHOUT tumor regression. The immune system is activated but cannot translate activation into tumor killing.
pharmacodynamic_dissociation.mechanism: In MSS CRC, the tumor microenvironment creates multiple barriers between activated T cells and tumor cells: (1) stromal barriers (TGF-β, CAFs, decorin) physically exclude T cells from the tumor center; (2) immunosuppressive cells (Tregs, MDSCs) suppress T cell activity at the tumor site; (3) tumor cells downregulate MHC-I to avoid T cell recognition. Vaccines and checkpoint inhibitors can activate T cells in the periphery but cannot overcome these barriers.
canonical_examples: GVAX: ORR 0% despite confirmed T-cell responses to vaccine antigens in peripheral blood
PolyPEPI1018: T-cell responses to ≥1 antigen in 100% of patients; no objective responses
PANVAC + DC vaccine: T-cell responses >70% of patients; no OS benefit
AMPLIFY-201: mKRAS-specific T-cell responses confirmed; ctDNA clearance in 3/5 — but no objective responses
pharmacodynamic_dissociation.crispro_implication: Pharmacodynamic dissociation is the dominant failure mode for vaccine programs in MSS CRC. The fix is not a better vaccine — it is a stromal barrier disruption strategy (TGF-β inhibition, anti-FAP, bevacizumab normalization) combined with the vaccine.

immune_exclusion.definition: T cells are present at the tumor invasive margin but cannot penetrate the tumor center due to stromal barriers.
immune_exclusion.mechanism: Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) secrete TGF-β, which: (1) induces epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) in tumor cells, increasing stromal density; (2) suppresses CD8+ T cell activity; (3) promotes regulatory T cell differentiation. Decorin and other extracellular matrix proteins create a physical barrier. The result: T cells accumulate at the tumor edge but cannot kill tumor cells at the center.
immune_exclusion.clinical_evidence: AtezoTRIBE: Immunoscore-IC high subgroup shows benefit (HR ~0.50) — these are the immune-inflamed patients. Immunoscore-IC low patients (immune excluded + immune desert) do not benefit.
immune_exclusion.crispro_implication: Immune exclusion is addressable — TGF-β inhibition (bintrafusp alfa, SHR-1701), anti-FAP agents, and bevacizumab (vascular normalization) can convert excluded tumors to inflamed. This is the rationale for combination strategies.

liver_metastasis_suppression.definition: Systemic immune suppression driven by liver metastases that prevents IO benefit even in patients with inflamed primary tumors.
liver_metastasis_suppression.mechanism: Liver metastases create a systemic immunosuppressive state through: (1) Kupffer cell PD-L1 expression and IL-10/TGF-β secretion; (2) recruitment of MDSCs and Tregs to the liver; (3) systemic elevation of immunosuppressive cytokines that suppress CD8+ T cell activity at all tumor sites. This is not a local effect — it suppresses immune responses throughout the body.
clinical_evidence: CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (IO HARMFUL) vs no-LM PFS HR=0.54; Pint=0.02
REGONIVO US: 0/5 liver-met responders; non-liver-met ORR 22%
REGONIVO Japan: all 8 CRC responders had NO liver metastases
liver_metastasis_suppression.crispro_implication: Liver metastasis is the single most important stratification variable for IO trials in MSS CRC. It is not just a prognostic factor — it is a predictive biomarker of IO resistance.

mek_inhibition_failure.definition: MEK inhibition was hypothesized to increase tumor immunogenicity in MSS CRC by upregulating MHC-I and reducing immunosuppressive cytokines. This hypothesis was definitively refuted.
mek_inhibition_failure.mechanism_hypothesis: MEK inhibition was expected to: (1) upregulate MHC-I expression on tumor cells; (2) reduce VEGF and immunosuppressive cytokine production; (3) sensitize tumors to checkpoint blockade. The hypothesis was based on preclinical data.
mek_inhibition_failure.why_it_failed: IMblaze370 (Phase III, N=363): OS no significant difference across all three arms (atezo+cobi vs atezo vs rego). MEK inhibition did not increase tumor immunogenicity in clinical MSS CRC. The preclinical-to-clinical translation failed (D7 — Translational). Additionally, cobimetinib (MEKi) suppressed T cell proliferation — the combination was potentially antagonistic.
mek_inhibition_failure.clinical_evidence: IMblaze370: OS atezo+cobi 8.87 months vs atezo 7.10 months vs rego 8.51 months — no significant difference. D1 Biology (0.9) — MEK inhibition definitively fails in MSS CRC.
mek_inhibition_failure.crispro_implication: MEK inhibition is not a viable IO sensitization strategy in MSS CRC. Any abstract proposing MEK+IO in MSS CRC should be flagged as repeating a definitively failed hypothesis.

program_IO_CORE.program_name: MSS CRC Immunotherapy Core Failure Corpus
program_IO_CORE.active_engagement: Brenus Pharma (BreAK CRC-001, STC-1010) — fit score 0.7375

trials.primary_result: OS HR 0.73 (90% CI 0.55–0.97, p=0.07); median OS 6.6 vs 4.1 months

D1_biology.rationale: PD-L1+CTLA-4 combination is biologically sound — dual checkpoint blockade.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Unselected MSS CRC population. pTMB ≥28 subgroup OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — the responder subgroup exists but was diluted. Liver-met patients (HR=1.39) were enrolled alongside NLM patients (HR=0.54), canceling the signal.

D6_interpretability.rationale: pTMB ≥28 threshold was data-driven (not pre-specified) — requires prospective validation.

responder_subpopulations.note: Strongest IO signal from randomized data in MSS CRC
IO is HARMFUL in liver-met patients

trials.transfer_lesson: Apply BOTH gates simultaneously: pTMB ≥28 AND non-liver-metastatic. The intersection of these two gates defines the IO-responsive subpopulation in MSS CRC.

trials.drug: Atezolizumab (PD-L1) + Cobimetinib (MEKi) vs Atezolizumab vs Regorafenib
trials.primary_result: OS: atezo+cobi 8.87 months vs atezo 7.10 months vs rego 8.51 months — no significant difference

D1_biology.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. MEK inhibition definitively fails to increase tumor immunogenicity in clinical MSS CRC. The preclinical hypothesis (MEKi upregulates MHC-I, reduces immunosuppressive cytokines) did not translate. Additionally, cobimetinib suppresses T cell proliferation — the combination may be antagonistic.

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected population — but even with selection, MEK inhibition is not a viable IO sensitization strategy.

D7_translational.rationale: Preclinical-to-clinical translation failure — MEKi immunogenicity effects seen in mouse models did not replicate in humans.

trials.transfer_lesson: MEK inhibition is NOT a viable IO sensitization strategy in MSS CRC. Any abstract proposing MEK+IO in MSS CRC is repeating a definitively failed hypothesis. Do not pursue.

trials.primary_result: ORR 0% (0/22 evaluable). Confirmed T-cell responses to vaccine antigens in peripheral blood.

D1_biology.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Pharmacodynamic dissociation — T cells activated in periphery but cannot kill tumor cells. The vaccine mechanism is biologically sound (T-cell activation confirmed) but the TME barriers prevent tumor killing. This is the canonical pharmacodynamic dissociation failure.

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected population — but even with selection, the stromal barrier problem remains.

D6_interpretability.rationale: T-cell responses confirmed but ORR 0% — the disconnect between immune activation and tumor regression is the key finding.

trials.key_finding: T-cell activation ≠ tumor regression in MSS CRC. The stromal barrier is the missing piece.
trials.transfer_lesson: Vaccine programs in MSS CRC must address the stromal barrier (TGF-β, CAFs) to convert immune activation into tumor killing. Vaccine alone is insufficient.

trials.primary_result: ITT PFS HR 0.69 (p=0.0075) — driven by MSI-H patients. pMMR subgroup PFS HR 0.86 (95% CI 0.63–1.17) — NOT significant

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. ITT benefit driven by MSI-H patients (dMMR). pMMR (MSS) subgroup shows no benefit (HR 0.86, not significant). Immunoscore-IC high subgroup shows directional benefit (HR ~0.50) — the immune-inflamed subpopulation within MSS CRC can benefit.

D1_biology.rationale: Atezolizumab mechanism is sound — PD-L1 blockade. The biology works in the right patient.

responder_subpopulations.note: Positive control — IO works in MSI-H
Unselected MSS CRC does not benefit

trials.transfer_lesson: In 1L MSS CRC, IO benefit requires Immunoscore-IC high (immune-inflamed TME). Unselected pMMR patients do not benefit. Liver metastasis interaction: AtezoTRIBE 2025 update shows Pint PFS=0.166 — liver metastasis is not predictive when Immunoscore-IC is high.

trials.drug: NANT vaccine cocktail + Nivolumab + mFOLFOX6 + Bevacizumab
trials.primary_result: PFS HR 1.061 (95% CI 0.380–2.966, p=0.91) — TERMINATED for futility

D1_biology.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Pharmacodynamic dissociation — vaccine activates T cells but cannot overcome MSS CRC TME barriers. The combination of vaccine + IO + chemo + bev is mechanistically complex but the fundamental problem (immune exclusion in MSS CRC) is not addressed.

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected MSS CRC — no liver metastasis stratification, no pTMB gate.

D3_architecture.rationale: Trial design precedent: adding IO to mFOLFOX6+bev in unselected MSS CRC does not improve PFS. This is the design precedent for BreAK CRC-001.

trials.transfer_lesson: QUILT-2.004 is the design precedent for BreAK CRC-001. Adding IO to mFOLFOX6+bev in unselected MSS CRC does not work. Patient selection (liver metastasis, pTMB) is mandatory.

trials.primary_result: HR 0.73 (90% CI 0.44–1.21) — CI crosses 1.0; data immature at bankruptcy. ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50

D2_selection.rationale: ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50 — directional benefit. ctDNA-low patients have lower disease burden and more intact immune function. This is a proxy for the immune-inflamed subpopulation.

D8_systemic.rationale: Gritstone Bio filed Chapter 11 October 2024 — data immature at bankruptcy. Results cannot be fully interpreted.

trials.transfer_lesson: ctDNA-low as a proxy for IO-responsive subpopulation in MSS CRC. Personalized neoantigen vaccines show directional benefit in ctDNA-low patients — this is the immune-inflamed subpopulation.

trials.primary_result: Median PFS 8.2 months (95% CI 5.9–8.6); ORR 64.5%; no randomized control arm

D3_architecture.rationale: PRIMARY LIMITATION. Single-arm design — cannot determine if IO adds benefit over mFOLFOX6 alone (TRIBE2 control arm: mPFS 9.8 months, ORR 50%). ORR 64.5% is higher than TRIBE2 ORR 50% but this may reflect patient selection (1L, good PS) rather than IO benefit.

D2_selection.rationale: CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup showed best outcomes — consistent with immune-inflamed hypothesis.

trials.transfer_lesson: MEDITREME ORR 64.5% vs TRIBE2 ORR 50% — directional signal but uncontrolled. The CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup finding supports the immune-inflamed hypothesis.

what_crispro_proved: THREE CONDITIONS for IO benefit in MSS CRC: non-liver-metastatic + pTMB ≥28 + immune-inflamed TME
CO.26: pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — strongest IO signal from randomized data
CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (HARMFUL) vs no-LM HR=0.54; Pint=0.02
REGONIVO US: ORR 7% overall; 0/5 liver-met responders; non-liver-met ORR 22%
GVAX: ORR 0% despite confirmed T-cell responses — pharmacodynamic dissociation
IMblaze370 (Phase III): OS no significant difference — MEK inhibition definitively fails
AtezoTRIBE: pMMR PFS HR=0.86 (NOT significant); Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 (exploratory)
QUILT-2.004: HR 1.061 (p=0.91) — IO addition to mFOLFOX6+bev fails in unselected MSS CRC

program_IO_APPENDIX.program_name: MSS CRC IO Supporting Evidence Corpus

trials.primary_result: PFS HR 0.60; OS HR 0.74. Definitive positive for MSI-H.
trials.role_in_corpus: POSITIVE CONTROL — confirms IO works in MSI-H CRC. Defines the contrast with MSS CRC.
trials.transfer_lesson: MSI-H CRC responds to IO (PFS HR 0.60). MSS CRC does not respond to IO in unselected populations. The difference is neoantigen load (high in MSI-H, low in MSS) and TME phenotype (inflamed in MSI-H, excluded/desert in MSS).

trials.primary_result: MSS cohort ORR 0% (n=74). MSI-H ORR 31% mono, 55% combo.
trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — confirms IO does NOT work in unselected MSS CRC. ORR 0% in 74 MSS patients.
trials.transfer_lesson: Unselected MSS CRC: ORR 0%. The responder subgroup (NLM + pTMB ≥28 + inflamed TME) is too small to detect in unselected populations.

trials.primary_result: ORR 7% (5/70). All 5 responders had NO liver metastases. Non-liver-met ORR 22% (5/23).
trials.role_in_corpus: LIVER METASTASIS EVIDENCE — confirms liver metastasis as the critical IO stratification variable
trials.transfer_lesson: 0/5 liver-met responders. Non-liver-met ORR 22%. Liver metastasis is a contraindication for IO in MSS CRC.

trials.primary_result: CRC cohort (n=24 MSS): ORR 33%, median PFS 6.3 months. All 8 responders had NO liver metastases.
trials.role_in_corpus: LIVER METASTASIS EVIDENCE — Japan cohort confirms NLM enrichment
trials.transfer_lesson: All 8 responders had NO liver metastases. ORR 33% in NLM-enriched cohort vs 7% in unselected US cohort.

trials.primary_result: ORR 27.6% overall; NLM cohort ORR ~40%; liver-met ORR ~5%. Median OS 20 months in NLM cohort.
trials.role_in_corpus: HIGHEST REPORTED IO ORR IN MSS CRC — NLM cohort ORR ~40%
trials.transfer_lesson: NLM cohort ORR ~40% is the highest reported IO ORR in MSS CRC. This defines the ceiling for IO benefit in the NLM subpopulation.

trials.primary_result: ORR 17% (95% CI 10–26%) in no-liver-met MSS mCRC (n=101)
trials.role_in_corpus: NLM ENRICHMENT EVIDENCE — confirms NLM selection strategy
trials.transfer_lesson: ORR 17% in NLM-selected MSS mCRC. Botensilimab's Fc-enhanced CTLA-4 blockade (increased ADCC/ADCP) may be more effective than standard CTLA-4 blockade in MSS CRC.

trials.primary_result: ONGOING — no results available
trials.role_in_corpus: ONGOING RANDOMIZED PHASE III — only ongoing randomized Phase III in refractory MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: STELLAR-303 is the only ongoing randomized Phase III in refractory MSS CRC. Results will define the new standard of care for 3L+ MSS mCRC.

trials.drug: mKRAS vaccine (ELI-002 2P) — lipid-conjugated KRAS G12D/G12V peptides
trials.primary_result: ctDNA clearance in 3/5 CRC patients. mKRAS-specific T-cell responses confirmed.
trials.role_in_corpus: PHARMACODYNAMIC DISSOCIATION EVIDENCE — T-cell responses confirmed but no objective responses
trials.transfer_lesson: mKRAS vaccine activates T cells and clears ctDNA but does not produce objective responses. Pharmacodynamic dissociation in MSS CRC.

trials.primary_result: T-cell responses to ≥1 antigen in 100% of patients. No objective responses. Stable disease in some patients.
trials.transfer_lesson: 100% T-cell response rate, 0% ORR. The most extreme pharmacodynamic dissociation example in the corpus.

trials.primary_result: PFS HR 1.04 (95% CI 0.77–1.40) — no benefit. OS HR 0.91 — no benefit. All biomarker subgroups negative.
trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — IO addition to 1L mFOLFOX6+bev fails in unselected population
trials.transfer_lesson: Confirms QUILT-2.004 finding: IO addition to mFOLFOX6+bev in unselected mCRC does not improve outcomes.

trials.primary_result: ORR 27.6% (8/29 evaluable). Median OS 9.1 months. Post-hoc: patients without liver metastases had better outcomes.
trials.role_in_corpus: LIVER METASTASIS EVIDENCE — post-hoc NLM benefit
trials.transfer_lesson: ORR 27.6% in small cohort — likely enriched for NLM patients. Post-hoc NLM benefit consistent with CO.26 and REGONIVO.

trials.primary_result: No T-cell increase; no objective responses.
trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — DNMT inhibition does not enhance GVAX in MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: Epigenetic priming (DNMT inhibition) does not overcome pharmacodynamic dissociation in MSS CRC.

trials.drug: PANVAC (CEA/MUC1 poxvirus vaccine) + Dendritic cell vaccine
trials.primary_result: No OS benefit; T-cell responses >70% of patients.
trials.transfer_lesson: T-cell responses >70%, no OS benefit. Pharmacodynamic dissociation confirmed across multiple vaccine platforms.

trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — oncolytic virus + IO fails in unselected MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: Oncolytic virus does not overcome immune exclusion in MSS CRC without patient selection.

trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — TGF-β + PD-L1 dual blockade fails in unselected MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: Even TGF-β + PD-L1 dual blockade fails in unselected MSS CRC. Patient selection (immune-excluded vs immune-desert) is required to identify patients who can benefit from stromal barrier disruption.

what_crispro_proved: RIN NLM subgroup: ORR ~40%, mOS 20 months — highest reported IO ORR in MSS CRC
BOT/BAL Phase 1: ORR 17% (95% CI 10–26%) in no-liver-met MSS mCRC (n=101)
KEYNOTE-177 (MSI-H): PFS HR=0.60 — positive control confirming IO works in MSI-H
CheckMate 142 MSS cohort: ORR 0% (n=74) — confirms immune exclusion in unselected MSS CRC
STELLAR-303 (zanzalintinib + atezolizumab): only ongoing randomized Phase III in refractory MSS CRC
Pharmacodynamic dissociation confirmed across 4 vaccine platforms (GVAX, PolyPEPI1018, PANVAC, AMPLIFY-201)
